old_value: {"id":"superintendent-uoy","title":"Implement status tracker fix (registration, liveness, tests)","description":"Three fixes from design spec: (1) register entries in run() after successful execution, (2) sandbox liveness check in check_agent_status(), (3) tests for registration, liveness, and round-trip","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-04-07T13:14:15Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-04-07T13:14:15Z"}

{"id":"superintendent-c67","title":"Enrich status command with git-level info and fix local lifecycle markers","description":"Two issues: (1) The status command only shows .ralph/ lifecycle markers which are useless for local targets since the terminal backend doesn't wrap with marker-writing shell. Fix: wrap local agent spawn with same lifecycle markers as docker backend. (2) The status command should show git-level info that smart_cleanup's analyze_entry() already computes: dirty/clean worktree, open/merged PR, unpushed commits, remote branch status. The infrastructure exists in analyze_entry() but isn't wired into the status command.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-04-07T17:28:41Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-04-07T17:28:41Z"}

{"id":"superintendent-388","title":"Audit test suite for mock-hiding-real-bugs patterns","description":"The status tracker investigation revealed that tests across the codebase use mocks that hide real bugs. Executor tests use MockHandler, integration tests use mock backends, and no tests verify state persistence end-to-end. Audit all test files and fix gaps: replace mocks with real backends where feasible, add state persistence assertions, test checkpoint resume, test concurrent registry access, test error recovery/rollback.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-04-07T14:34:18Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-04-07T14:34:18Z"}

{"id":"superintendent-760","title":"Unify run and resume: reuse existing worktrees","description":"superintendent run should reuse existing worktrees instead of always creating new ones. Current behavior: run always calls git worktree add -b which fails if branch/worktree exists. resume exists as a separate command but doesn't spawn an agent. Proposed behavior for run: (1) worktree + branch exist → skip creation, spawn agent (2) branch exists but no worktree → git worktree add \u003cpath\u003e \u003cbranch\u003e without -b (3) neither exists → current behavior with -b. The resume command can become a thin convenience wrapper or be removed. The --force flag should handle nuke-and-recreate.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:46:28Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:46:28Z"}

{"id":"superintendent-3ie","title":"Investigate beads post-checkout hook failing git worktree add","description":"When creating a worktree with git worktree add, the beads post-checkout hook runs and fails if the Dolt database name doesnt match (e.g. database from a previous worktree prefix). This causes git worktree add to return non-zero even though the worktree was successfully created. superintendent sees the non-zero exit and reports failure. Need to investigate: (1) should the post-checkout hook be more resilient to missing databases (2) should superintendent ignore hook failures during worktree creation (3) should worktree creation use core.hooksPath=/dev/null","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"brendan.whitney@faraday.io","created_at":"2026-03-14T22:46:33Z","created_by":"Brendan Whitney","updated_at":"2026-03-14T22:46:33Z"}
